title: Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves
tagline: Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Udayagiri and Khandagiri caves, formerly called Kattaka Gumpha or Cuttack caves, are partly natural and partly artificial caves of archaeological, historical, and religious importa
overview.description: Udayagiri and Khandagiri caves, formerly called Kattaka Gumpha or Cuttack caves, are partly natural and partly artificial caves of archaeological, historical, and religious importance, located 3 km south of the city of Bhubaneswar in Odisha, India. The caves are situated on two adjacent hills, Udayagiri and Khandagiri mentioned as Kumari Parvata in the Hathigumpha inscription. They have a number of finely and ornately carved caves built during the 2nd or 1st century BCE. It is believed that most of these caves were carved out as residential blocks for Jain monks during the reign of King Kharav

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Hathigumpha inscription
topPlaces.description: The Hathigumpha Inscription (pronounced: ɦɑːt̪ʰiːgumpʰɑː) is a Jain inscription consisting of seventeen lines written in Prakrit language incised in Brahmi script in a cavern called Hathigumpha in Udayagiri hills, near Bhubaneswar in Odisha, India. Dated between the second century BCE and the first century CE, it was inscribed by the Jain king Kharavela of the Kalinga kingdom.

topPlaces.name: Digambara Jain temple, Khandagiri
topPlaces.description: Digambara Jaina Temple is a Jain temple in Bhubaneswar, in the state of Odisha, India. The temple is on the top of Khandagiri hill.

topPlaces.name: Siksha 'O' Anusandhan
topPlaces.description: Siksha 'O' Anusandhan (SOA), formerly Siksha 'O' Anusandhan University (SOA University) (transl. Education and Research University (ERU)), is a private deemed university in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. Many of the programs are nationally accredited, such as engineering, medicine, pharmacy, business, nursing, biotechnology, science, humanities, environment, nano technology, materials science, agriculture and law.

topPlaces.name: Parsvanath Jain Temple-I
topPlaces.description: Parsvanath Jain Temple – I is located in Digambara Jain Temple precinct in Khandagiri, Bhubaneswar. It is a living temple.

topPlaces.name: Parsvanath Jain Temple-II
topPlaces.description: Parsvanath Jain Temple – II is located in Bhubaneswar. This temple is located in the Digambar Jain Temple precinct in Khandagiri.

topPlaces.name: Odisha Crafts Museum
topPlaces.description: Odisha Crafts Museum also known as Kalabhoomi designed by Architects' Studio, is a museum in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India, dedicated to the art and crafts of Odisha. It was inaugurated by the Chief Minister of Odisha, Naveen Patnaik, on 22 March 2018.

topPlaces.name: Lok Bhavan, Bhubaneswar
topPlaces.description: Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan of Bhubaneswar is the official residence of the Governor of Odisha. It is located in the city of Bhubaneswar, Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Odisha
topPlaces.description: Odisha (Oriya: oṛiśā, pronounced [oˈɽisa] ), spelled Orissa until 2011, is a state located in eastern India. It is the eighth-largest state by area, and the eleventh-largest by population, with over 41 million inhabitants.

items.note: The Hathigumpha Inscription (pronounced: ɦɑːt̪ʰiːgumpʰɑː) is a Jain inscription consisting of seventeen lines written in Prakrit language incised in Brahmi script in a cavern called Hathigumpha in Udayagiri hills, near Bhubaneswar in Odisha, India. Dated between the second century BCE and the first century CE, it was inscribed by the Jain king Kharavela of the Kalinga kingdom.
Digambara Jaina Temple is a Jain temple in Bhubaneswar, in the state of Odisha, India. The temple is on the top of Khandagiri hill.

Siksha 'O' Anusandhan (SOA), formerly Siksha 'O' Anusandhan University (SOA University) (transl. Education and Research University (ERU)), is a private deemed university in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India. Many of the programs are nationally accredited, such as engineering, medicine, pharmacy, business, nursing, biotechnology, science, humanities, environment, nano technology, materials science, agriculture and law.
Parsvanath Jain Temple – I is located in Digambara Jain Temple precinct in Khandagiri, Bhubaneswar. It is a living temple.
Parsvanath Jain Temple – II is located in Bhubaneswar. This temple is located in the Digambar Jain Temple precinct in Khandagiri.

Odisha Crafts Museum also known as Kalabhoomi designed by Architects' Studio, is a museum in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India, dedicated to the art and crafts of Odisha. It was inaugurated by the Chief Minister of Odisha, Naveen Patnaik, on 22 March 2018.
Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan of Bhubaneswar is the official residence of the Governor of Odisha. It is located in the city of Bhubaneswar, Odisha.

Odisha (Oriya: oṛiśā, pronounced [oˈɽisa] ), spelled Orissa until 2011, is a state located in eastern India. It is the eighth-largest state by area, and the eleventh-largest by population, with over 41 million inhabitants.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves?
faq.a: The best time to visit Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (4 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves?
faq.a: Stays in Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Udayagiri and Khandagiri Caves?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hathigumpha inscription, Digambara Jain temple, Khandagiri, Siksha 'O' Anusandhan, Parsvanath Jain Temple-I, Parsvanath Jain Temple-II.
